---
title: "Week 12 — Traditional inference as shortcut"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

How do formulas approximate simulation?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Sampling distributions and the approximation logic; means and
proportions with standard errors as shortcuts for the simulation
results from weeks 10–11.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Read a one-sample or two-sample t/z test output and write a
conclusion in context.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new test output;
estimation-vs-testing application.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 13 (foundations-mathematical), Ch 19 (one-mean),
Ch 20 (two-means), and Ch 21 (paired) — selected items.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Bridging language back to weeks 10–11 (randomization / bootstrap)
to frame formulas as shortcuts, not the intellectual center.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Recognize traditional tests as shortcuts to uncertainty
      reasoning.
- [ ] Interpret standard error conceptually.
- [ ] Read common test output.
- [ ] Connect traditional inference to prior simulation logic.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
